jokes.text: Stupid. Stupid chair. Very stupid chair.
jokes.setup: Michael is having trouble with a piece of furniture while trying to appear competent to his son
jokes.punchline: The increasingly frustrated repetition reveals his childish tantrum over an inanimate object
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy in the escalating repetition, shows Michael's frustration with his circumstances through a simple object

jokes.text: Yeah, but isn't that why Grandpa's in jail, because he took things from work?
jokes.setup: Michael justifies taking a chair from work because he's president
jokes.punchline: George Michael's innocent logic exposes the hypocrisy - this is exactly what got Grandpa arrested
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - George Michael's moral clarity contrasts perfectly with Michael's rationalization, and it's plot-relevant

jokes.text: Leather chair? So, you're against stealing, but skinning cows is cool with you.
jokes.setup: Michael wants a leather chair after lecturing about stealing
jokes.punchline: George Michael points out the moral inconsistency in a deadpan way
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor but feels slightly forced compared to the previous stealing joke

jokes.text: I'm fine, by the way. Frankly, your concern is getting embarrassing.
jokes.setup: No one has shown any concern for George Michael
jokes.punchline: His sarcastic complaint about excessive concern that doesn't exist
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of a relatable frustration - being ignored while family drama unfolds

jokes.text: Neither would I. What career?
jokes.setup: Marta says she would never belittle Gob's career
jokes.punchline: Michael immediately does exactly that with perfect timing
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and economy - Michael's dismissiveness comes through in just two words after agreeing not to belittle

jokes.text: Oh, the tricks. The little tricks.
jokes.setup: Marta mentions Gob's magic career
jokes.punchline: Michael's condescending diminishment gets progressively worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Michael's dismissiveness, showing his inability to be supportive

jokes.text: Él es zombi!
jokes.setup: Gob performs his needle-through-neck trick for children
jokes.punchline: The children are genuinely terrified, screaming that he's a zombie
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy that shows how inappropriate Gob's magic is for the audience

jokes.text: Oh, sure. First you dump all over it, now you wanna know how it's done.
jokes.setup: Marta is horrified by Gob terrifying children
jokes.punchline: Gob completely misreads her reaction as professional interest
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob characterization - his narcissism makes him unable to read genuine horror

jokes.text: the foyer or the kitchen.
jokes.setup: Michael says he hasn't seen Gob 'here'
jokes.punchline: He clarifies he means only specific rooms, implying Gob IS staying there
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection that shows Michael's attempt to technically tell the truth while lying

jokes.text: The loser. The fool.
jokes.setup: Lindsay calls Michael 'the noble one' and 'the one who never wins'
jokes.punchline: She escalates to increasingly harsh descriptors
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing Lindsay's casual cruelty disguised as observation

jokes.text: Your legs look exactly like mine, and I just shaved mine.
jokes.setup: George Michael is trying to appear more masculine to Maeby
jokes.punchline: She points out his legs look like a woman's freshly shaved legs
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures teenage awkwardness and George Michael's failed attempts at masculinity

jokes.text: I'm gonna need a leather jacket when I'm on my hog and need to go into a controlled slide.
jokes.setup: George Michael wants to appear tough by getting a motorcycle
jokes.punchline: His overly technical explanation of why he'd need protective gear
jokes.explanation: Shows George Michael's nerdy approach to trying to be cool - he researches safety equipment

jokes.text: But you have every day off. You don't have a job.
jokes.setup: Tobias announces he has the entire day off
jokes.punchline: Maeby points out the obvious - he's unemployed
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response that deflates Tobias's attempt to sound important

jokes.text: Which brother?
jokes.setup: Mysterious man says 'If you care about your brother, get in this car'
jokes.punchline: Michael's practical clarification about which brother needs help
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Michael's practical nature even in threatening situations

jokes.text: She changed him as a baby.
jokes.setup: Lucille objects to Buster dating her friend
jokes.punchline: She reveals the deeply inappropriate nature of their relationship
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical way to make the relationship extremely creepy with just five words

jokes.text: That's why she didn't look surprised.
jokes.setup: Learning that Lucille 2 changed Buster as a baby
jokes.punchline: Michael realizes this explains her lack of surprise at seeing Buster naked
jokes.explanation: Clever callback that retroactively explains an earlier moment while adding to the creepiness

jokes.text: Okay. That-That's about the creepiest thing I've ever heard.
jokes.setup: Lucille's baby-changing revelation
jokes.punchline: Michael's stunned reaction speaking for the audience
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat that voices what everyone is thinking. Michael as audience surrogate works here.

jokes.text: You're the only child who chose a spouse I liked, and she's the one who had to die.
jokes.setup: Lucille is complaining about her children's relationship choices
jokes.punchline: She reveals her selfish perspective on Michael's wife's death
jokes.explanation: Shockingly selfish perspective that perfectly captures Lucille's character - making her son's tragedy about her

jokes.text: It's a good thing he's already got that little scooter.
jokes.setup: Michael warns that people want to break Gob's legs
jokes.punchline: Lucille immediately finds the silver lining in her son's potential injury
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Lucille's callousness - finding practical advantages to her son being crippled

jokes.text: I guess you'll just have to decide which Lucille you want to spend your nights with.
jokes.setup: Lucille has removed Buster's bed to force him to choose
jokes.punchline: She frames it as choosing between her and his girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Shows Lucille's manipulative nature and the creepy dynamics of the situation

jokes.text: Sounds a little bit like 'dating Mom.'
jokes.setup: Buster says he'll continue dating
jokes.punchline: He accidentally reveals the Freudian nature of his relationship choice
jokes.explanation: Buster accidentally articulates the psychological issues everyone can see but won't say

jokes.text: I think you're confusing scary with silly. This guy was frightening. He was in a limo.
jokes.setup: Gob says Rollo is a magician, not a mobster
jokes.punchline: Michael's evidence for scariness is having a limo
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's inability to grasp that successful magicians can be threatening

jokes.text: The legs... The bottom half of my saw-the-lady-in-half trick.
jokes.setup: Rollo wants to 'take' Gob's legs
jokes.punchline: Gob reveals 'legs' refers to his magic assistant, not body parts
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection that recontextualizes the entire threatening situation

jokes.text: God, I gotta stop giving these things away.
jokes.setup: Gob has just explained how the saw trick works
jokes.punchline: He realizes he's revealing trade secrets while explaining
jokes.explanation: Shows Gob's lack of self-control and professional awareness

jokes.text: The old top half quit when she found out I was sleeping with the legs.
jokes.setup: Explanation of why Gob needs a new assistant
jokes.punchline: He's having an affair with half of a magic trick
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd situation that treats magic assistants like workplace drama

jokes.text: The legs are insanely jealous.
jokes.setup: Gob is explaining his relationship drama with his magic assistants
jokes.punchline: He attributes human emotions to 'the legs'
jokes.explanation: Escalates the absurdity by giving 'legs' personality traits and emotions

jokes.text: She doesn't respect my career. / Okay.
jokes.setup: Gob complaining about Marta not respecting his magic
jokes.punchline: Michael's exhausted, defeated 'Okay' acknowledging the absurdity
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man reaction. Michael's single word conveys complete exasperation with Gob's delusions.

jokes.text: Well, you get a false feeling of superiority.
jokes.setup: Michael complains about getting nothing from being moral
jokes.punchline: George Michael accurately identifies what Michael does get from it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character insight - George Michael sees through his father's self-righteousness

jokes.text: Hey, guys. Oh, that's why.
jokes.setup: Michael asks why he has to set a good example
jokes.punchline: George Michael arrives, immediately answering the question
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual comedy timing - the answer to Michael's question literally walks in

jokes.text: Moo? Mmm. Moo, honey.
jokes.setup: Lindsay and Maeby are wearing all leather
jokes.punchline: They're making cow sounds while covered in leather
jokes.explanation: Obvious visual gag that lacks the sophistication of better AD jokes

jokes.text: I can't say how or when... or where my wedding ring is...
jokes.setup: Tobias has money to go shopping
jokes.punchline: He implies he sold his wedding ring without directly saying it
jokes.explanation: Shows Tobias's financial desperation while maintaining plausible deniability

jokes.text: Something that says, 'leather daddy'?
jokes.setup: Tobias wants something that says 'Dad likes leather'
jokes.punchline: The clerk mishears it as a sexual term
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for Tobias's unintentional sexual innuendo, became a running gag

jokes.text: Oh, is there such a thing?
jokes.setup: Clerk mentions 'leather daddy'
jokes.punchline: Tobias's innocent interest in what he thinks is a clothing category
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias obliviousness to sexual context, delivered with complete innocence

jokes.text: Well, you can't change who you are.
jokes.setup: Marta calling him a good guy after he's plotting against her
jokes.punchline: Michael ironically states the show's theme while actively contradicting it
jokes.explanation: Great thematic irony. Michael states the central premise while actively trying to change who he is.

jokes.text: Gothic Asshole? That's what I said.
jokes.setup: Tobias asks to go to Gothic Castle
jokes.punchline: Driver mishears and Tobias agrees, not realizing the insult
jokes.explanation: Clever mishearing that leads to Tobias accidentally agreeing to be called an asshole

jokes.text: Oh, I am glad I didn't go with that outfit.
jokes.setup: Tobias ends up at what appears to be a leather bar/club instead of magic venue
jokes.punchline: He's relieved about his outfit choice while completely missing the context
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy of Tobias being oblivious to being in an inappropriate location

jokes.text: You're no more in love with him than I am.
jokes.setup: Lucille 2 claims to love Buster
jokes.punchline: Lucille accidentally reveals her own feelings
jokes.explanation: Freudian slip that reveals Lucille's own inappropriate feelings

jokes.text: I'm gonna fill that hole, 'cause we're in love.
jokes.setup: Lucille mentioning Buster was born with a hole in his heart
jokes.punchline: Lucille 2's response with unfortunate sexual implications
jokes.explanation: Awkward phrasing in context of their age gap relationship. The double meaning creates uncomfortable comedy.

jokes.text: You've stolen so many things from me over the years. This is the first time you've ever given something back.
jokes.setup: Gob gives Michael a watch
jokes.punchline: Michael's backhanded way of accepting a gift
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael - can't accept kindness without cataloguing past grievances

jokes.text: So what? So I don't have any hair on my arms or legs. So what?
jokes.setup: George Michael has given up trying to appear tough
jokes.punchline: He defiantly embraces his lack of body hair
jokes.explanation: Nice character arc resolution where George Michael accepts himself

jokes.text: I'm not that nice.
jokes.setup: George Michael calls his dad a 'boring, old nice guy'
jokes.punchline: Michael's weak protest while planning to betray his brother
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony as Michael protests his niceness while executing a betrayal plan

jokes.text: Where am I gonna get a pair of beautiful women's legs on such short notice?
jokes.setup: Michael needing to solve Gob's missing 'legs' problem
jokes.punchline: His question that sounds inappropriate but refers to magic trick
jokes.explanation: Good double entendre. Sounds like Michael is being creepy but he's genuinely solving a magic trick problem.

jokes.text: Wiggle those sexy gams, lady.
jokes.setup: Gob is performing magic with what he thinks are 'the legs'
jokes.punchline: He makes inappropriate comments to Marta during the trick
jokes.explanation: Shows Gob's inappropriate behavior while treating his girlfriend like his affair partner

jokes.text: You guys are friends again. You can play bridge together.
jokes.setup: The two Lucilles are embracing and making up
jokes.punchline: Buster completely misses that they're bonding over excluding him
jokes.explanation: Buster's obliviousness to his girlfriends bonding by rejecting him

jokes.text: Could you just... just separate?
jokes.setup: The two Lucilles embracing dramatically
jokes.punchline: Buster's uncomfortable request for them to stop
jokes.explanation: Buster's discomfort at their reunion. His awkward phrasing makes it funnier.

jokes.text: This really worked better with the chair.
jokes.setup: Michael is trying to recline on the broken model home furniture
jokes.punchline: He realizes his scheme was pointless - the chair he wanted wouldn't help anyway
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows the futility of Michael's desires and schemes

jokes.text: That was just my girlfriend and my nephew.
jokes.setup: Alliance asks how Gob got two approved assistants
jokes.punchline: Gob's casual revelation that he used non-professionals
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob - he accidentally confesses to breaking the rules while trying to take credit

jokes.text: I got a gig.
jokes.setup: Tobias announces good news about getting work
jokes.punchline: Context implies he got hired at the leather bar he accidentally visited
jokes.explanation: Payoff to the misdirection plot - Tobias's obliviousness leads to employment
